name.markdownDescription:
provides a logical name for this aggregator (e.g., 'errors').
This value is accessible at run-time via the intrinsic 'inputName' field
to identify tenxSummaries originating from this aggregator.

enabled.markdownDescription:
sets whether to open the input stream.

To enable this input only when an 'aggregateErrors' startup argument value is truthy:

``` yaml
...
aggregator:
  enabled: $=TenXEnv.get("aggregateErrors")
``` (Default: true)

fields.description: list of field names by which to aggregate tenxObjects
fields.markdownDescription:
provides a list of intrinsic/extracted/calculated [tenxObject](http://doc.log10x.com/api/js/#TenXObject) field names by which
to aggregate instances.

defaultValue.description: a constant value to default if 'maxCardinality' is exceeded
defaultValue.markdownDescription:
defines a constant value to use instead of [field values](#aggregatorfields)
when the current number of rows within the aggregator's in-mem table during
[flushInterval](#aggregatorflushinterval) exceeds [maxCardinality](#aggregatormaxcardinality).

This value prevents an out-of-mem error if tenxObjects whose [field values](#aggregatorfields)
are of high cardinality, which can cause the in-mem table to grow beyond the
confines of the host JVM heap.

This error is possible if [fields](#aggregatorfields) contains a calculated/extracted field
whose value is alphanumeric (e.g., 'spanID') and is practically unique to each tenxObject.

totalFields.description: list of fields whose values to tally into generated tenxSummaries
totalFields.markdownDescription:
specifies a list of intrinsic calculated/extracted tenxObject field names whose value is extracted from
each instance processed by the aggregator and added to its associated in-mem table row.

When the aggregator flushes the in-mem table to produced tenxSummaries, each generated tenxSummary instance provides access to the total values
via its [summaryTotals](http://doc.log10x.com/api/js/#TenXSummary+summaryTotals) array field.

This option allows aggregators to tally and report on a custom value extracted
from aggregated tenxObject instances (e.g., 'price') within a target flush [threshold](#aggregatorflushthreshold) or [interval](#aggregatorflushinterval).

maxCardinality.description: max number of concurrent rows within the aggregator in-mem table within the flush interval
maxCardinality.markdownDescription:
sets the maximum number of unique rows within the aggregator's in-mem table.
If the number of rows exceeds this value during [flushInterval](#aggregatorflushinterval) , the 'aggregatorDefaultValue'
value will be for all tenxObjects instead of [field values](#aggregatorfields) until the aggregator is flushed.

This value is used as a backstop to ensure that the aggregator's in-mem table
does not overflow when extracting and  aggregating very high cardinality values
from the fields of tenxObjects passed to this aggregator.

If no value is provided or set to 0, the aggregator has no cap. This may result
in an out-of-mem error when very high cardinality (e.g., 'spanID', 'GUID') [fields](#aggregatorfields)
are specified.

filter.description: a JavaScript expression an tenxObject must evaluate as 'truthy' against to be aggregated
filter.markdownDescription:
provides a way to control which tenxObjects are incorporated
into this aggregator. Unlike the 'aggregatorEnabled', which enables/disables this aggregator
altogether, this JavaScript expression is evaluated per tenxObject to provide
granular control over what information to aggregate.

For example, to only aggregate tenxObjects read from a specified input:

``` yaml
aggregator:
  filter: this.inputName == "elastic"
```

To apply more complex logic, invoke an l1x JavaScript function declared in a .js file by passing it to the 10x runtime
at startup (e.g., $ l1x run @my.js).

``` yaml
aggregator:
  ...
  filter: myAggregatorFilter()
```

For an example function, see [policy/actions.js](https://github.com/log-10x/config/blob/main/pipelines/run/modules/receive/policy/actions.js).

flushThreshold.description: maximum the number of tenxObjects to aggregate before the aggregator flushes
flushThreshold.markdownDescription:
controls how many tenxObjects to upsert into this aggregator (e.g., 10000) before the
aggregator transforms the rows of its in-mem table into tenxSummaries instances and flushes them into the pipeline.

If not set, the [flushInterval](#aggregatorflushinterval) argument determines
when to flush the aggregator. If neither argument is present, the aggregator
only flushes pending tenxSummaries when the pipeline terminates.

flushInterval.description: interval to wait before the aggregator flushes
flushInterval.markdownDescription:
sets the period (e.g., '30s'), after which the aggregator transforms the rows of its in-mem table into
l1xSummaries instances and flushes them into the pipeline.

If set, the [flushThreshold](#aggregatorflushthreshold)  argument determines
when to flush the aggregator. If neither argument is present, the aggregator
only flushes pending tenxSummaries when the pipeline terminates.
